Content: Spanish Folklore; Lore of Holland. Side I: Comparisons and Proverbs; Comparisons and Proverbs. Side II: 10 Songs by Avelina Castillo [end Spanish Folk Speech]; Lore of Holland: Nichlas - song and customs associated with it; The Fifth of December - song; Santa Claus - song; Rhyme in Dutch - similar to Humpty Dumpty; Rhyme - about a little girl who went to the pawn shop and pawned her coat and then went to buy a lot of things; Discussion About People who Wear Wooden Shoes and Why; Song- about a mother's wish that her son will grow up to be a Captain; Pithain - song about the boy who brought in the Silver Fleet; Lullaby; Story about Hans and Fitche (Hansel and Gretal); National Holidays; Hide and Seek - game; Bim Bom Beya - counting out rhyme; Vie Vie Vye Vecht - counting out rhyme Green Pea Soup - how it is made; Ginger bread - how it is made; Meat Loaf - how it is made; Unbaked Apple Pie - how it is made; Frying Fish; The National Anthem of Holland
Title from tape: Spanish Folk Speech * Louise Demont

medium_tesim: sound tape reels : 7 1/2 ips, two track, mono.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Ethnomusicology Archive
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Archive of California and Western Folklore
description_tesim: Collected by: Mrs. Anne M. Evans

1. Clover blossoms used to cure skin diseases;2. Mutton tallow used to cure sore hands;3. Beef's gall and alcohol used to cure cuts, infections;4. Turpentine on sugar used to cure colic. (appendicitis);5. Snake's head plant used to cure worms. (tapeworms);6. Motherwort plant used for females ailments;7. White and red clover used to cure pimples;8. Cure for worms contributed Mrs. Evans;9. Pennyroyal and peppermint plant used to cure fevers;10. Wild cherry tonic;11. Leeches;12. Ground hemlock used to restore hair;13. Hot poultices used for toothaches;14. Hops grown around privies;15. Ringworms cured by sulphur put on cold steel;16. Mud used to cure beestings;17. Long life in her family. lack of sanitation;18. Talk about her relatives in Nova Scotia;19. Salt herring filets bound on feet to cure colds;20. Tea leaves used to cure styes;21. Bread poultices used to cure boils on fingers;22. Skin from eggshell used to draw boils;23. Pneumonia cured by cornmeal and onion poultices;24. Black flies (Spanish) bound over heart to cure Mrs. Carey's heart ailment (used by a doctor!).

medium_tesim: sound tape reels : 7 1/2 ips, two track, mono.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Ethnomusicology Archive
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Archive of California and Western Folklore
description_tesim: Collected by: Mrs. Anne M. Evans

1. Why the Micmacs have small families;2. Poland Spring Water. Its cure for gall stones. Its use by Indians;3. Talk about her granddaughter Mary;4. A canoe trip;5. Indian hunting skill;6. Intermarriage of Indians and whites. Deterioration of the Indian tribes. The extinction of the Passamaquoddy;7. Indians around N. Andover, Mass;8. Mrs. Carey teaches at an Indian school.

Side II Strike Music of American Labor: Hallelujah I'm a Bum (Greenway?); Pie in the Sky; Solidarity Forever; Union Maid - (a dub of the Almanac Singers record); Talking Union - (a dub of the Almanac Singers record); Sister Jenny's Turn to Throw the Ball; Ludlow Massacre - Woody Guthrie; Which Side - Almanacs; Join the NMU; Harry Sims; Fight for Union Recognition (tune: I've been workig on the railroad); The Boatmakers' Union is a No Good Union. Jane Fitzpatrick: An Ould Mon Came A-Courtin'; Oh Dear, What Shall Ee Dae; The Drowned Lover; Unnamed Tune.

Performers: Jane Fitzpatrick, Sam (miner, last name unknown).

description_tesim: Performer : "Kolo" Serbian State Folk Ensemble; Monire Vakili; Hossein Dehlavi; Orchestra of Fine Arts Administration of Iran; Ali Golab
Title from tape: Iranian & Serbian Recordings

Side I - Serbija; Igre 3 Servije (Dances from Serbia): Poskok; Durdevka; Igrale Se Delije; Dances from Pirot (E. Serbia); Sopsko Oro and Vlainica; Kacerac (with frula); Caj. Goro Carna (Hail Black Mountain); Ojimoja Ruzo Rumena (Oh, My Red Rose); Kraj Vardara; Gajde (dance); (Jugoton - LPM6 - (Zagreb, Ilica 213)). Yugoslav Folk Songs and Dances: Serbian Fo; Side II - Iran; 3 arranged folk songs: Reihane (Kurdish); Karwane (Kurdish); Shir Ali Mardan (Dezful); Katuli - Mazanderan (E. Caspian Province, accompanied by ney); Shor Shor (Kurdestan, W. Iran); Amina Way (Kurdestan, W. Iran); Giyan Giyan (Kurdestan, W. Iran); Bridal Song (Dezful, S. W. Iran); 45 rpm recordings Leyla Jan (Sistan, S. E. Iran); Sardar Amad Be Sistan (Sistan, S. E. Iran); Kharom Sharsharia; Garan Chapi Sorna - Dohol; Bivasa Donya

description_tesim: Title from tape: Ann Wheeler * Joan O'Connor

Copy of Joan O'Connor tape - from Horse Cave, Kentucky. The tape was recorded in Kentucky and sent to Mrs. O'Connor - Folklore 105 & 106. Informants identified on transcriptions in UCLA Archive - backgrounds also given.

Capture and Escape of Uncle Raz Smith from Beaumont, Kentucky;Mr. Dugan Slinker of Metcalf County, Kentucky;The Lay Preacher;Brother London at the Revival;The Preacher Stung by a Wasp;Dugan Trying to Get Religion;The Balcony on the Bijou;True Story of Robbers;William P. Davis' Black Tennant;Rapid Strides;Over There (in that land so bright and fair).

description_tesim: Title from tape: Pursell * Russian & Jewish Songs
Performers: Mrs. Tillie Pursell; Mr. Herschel Slavin.

Mrs. Tillie Pursell:The Rabbie Does Wonders - Jewish;Don't Worry My Dear - Russian (tongue in cheeck song) (melody like Norweigian "The Younger Generation";Play, Play, Be Gay - Jewish (Resembles "Hullabaloo Belay").Mr. Herschel Slavin:Forgive My Sins - Hebrew;Hebrew Chant - Hebrew;Hebrew Chant - Hebrew (The singer paces as he sings and at one point walked out of the range of the microphone.).Mrs. Tillie Pursell:Listen My Pupils - Jewish;The Jew is Crying - Jewish (singer could not remember third stanza);The Czar's Manifesto - Russian (words listed under Underground Activities in collector's notes);Teaching the Children Their A B Cs - Jewish;I Left My Home for Israel - Yiddish and Hebrew (singer had difficulty in remembering the words);Dark Eyes Have Fire - Jewish;Two White Doves are on My Window -  Jewish (love song);Lullaby - Russian (Traditional);I Only Have One Dime and That Dime's Not Mine - Hebrew;The Bird's Message - Jewish;Bim Bam Bom - Jewish (Passing of the Sabbath);The Scholar's Dream - Jewish (singer's comment - It's rather Faust like);Where Were You When Life was Sweet? - Jewish;Where the Girls Go, I Go - Russian (soldier's song) (singer could not remember the chorus);Oh Rabbi Tell Me - Russian-Jewish (written by Frugg);The Preacher's Dog - Russian (nonsense);The Harvest - Russian-Jewish (written by Frugg).
All titles are merely for general content.  They are not the titles that are associated with the songs because the singer could not recall them.

repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Ethnomusicology Archive
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Archive of California and Western Folklore
description_tesim: Collected by: Mrs. Anne M. Evans

1. String of red coral beads worn around neck to cure nosebleed;2. Talk about her relatives;3. Belief that screens give "strained air." Talk about screens;4. Pierced ears improve eyesight;5. White rose petals cured with sugar used for sore throats;6. Red sumac syrup used to cure coughs;7. Warts given by toads, frogs, beavers;8. Warts cured by raw chicken put in crotch of tree in dark of moon. When it went away, the warts went away;9. Saliva used to cure warts;10. Old shoe or mocassin chewed for stomach ailments;11. Croup cured by drinking mother's hot urine. (She read article about this cure later);12. Cow manure poultices of pigeon dung used for cures;13. Tobacco poultices used for many things;14. "Commercial travellers" who sold medicines. Carter's Little Liver Pills;15. Jack the Ripper in Nova Scotia;16. Talk about her uncle, Capt. John Smith. His murder;17. Talk about her mother's father. The underground railway.

Content: Spanish Folklore; Lore of Holland. Side I: Comparisons and Proverbs; Comparisons and Proverbs. Side II: 10 Songs by Avelina Castillo [end Spanish Folk Speech]; Lore of Holland: Nichlas - song and customs associated with it; The Fifth of December - song; Santa Claus - song; Rhyme in Dutch - similar to Humpty Dumpty; Rhyme - about a little girl who went to the pawn shop and pawned her coat and then went to buy a lot of things; Discussion About People who Wear Wooden Shoes and Why; Song- about a mother's wish that her son will grow up to be a Captain; Pithain - song about the boy who brought in the Silver Fleet; Lullaby; Story about Hans and Fitche (Hansel and Gretal); National Holidays; Hide and Seek - game; Bim Bom Beya - counting out rhyme; Vie Vie Vye Vecht - counting out rhyme Green Pea Soup - how it is made; Ginger bread - how it is made; Meat Loaf - how it is made; Unbaked Apple Pie - how it is made; Frying Fish; The National Anthem of Holland

Pretty Polly (Polly Oliver);Lightning Express - fragment;True and Trembling Brakeman - fragment;Lord Bateman - fragment and prose summary;Two Brothers - fragment;Lone Pilgrim;Rank Stranger;Cole Younger;Brother Green ("That's old Civil War and my mother said/that/indicates her mother was 5 or 7 when war broke out -- but was born in 1868/ she remembers -- she was standing at a little cleft tree," "soldiers passed by her beating a drum -- and she heard someone singing the song.  This passage is not clear.  - Apparently her mother (who may have been born in 1858) was standing by a cleft tree or bush when someone came by playing fifes and drums.  The patted her on the head.  Then she moved somewhere else and heard the song, and the people who sang the song were somehow related to the brother in the song.;Rough and Rowdy Boys;Utah Carl - Learned from her brother in Little Rock about 40 years ago.;Tramp in the Rain/Can I Sleep in your Barn Tonight, Mister?;Lilla Lee - learned from mother;Charming Beauty Bright - learned from mother;"At the foot of yonders mountain where there runs a clear stream" - from mother;Cottage on a Hillside - read text in a book about 1919 - set it to a tune herself.;Three Little Babes (Wife of Usher's Well);The Little Family (incomplete?)

description_tesim: Performers: Floyd W. Moore; Joseph Fulcher
Title from tape: Navy Lore - Fulcher - Moore - Halley

Gold Earrings;Traditions, changes and the uses of the Navy uniform;Informant information, ranks, ribbons, and more about uniforms;Comments on happenings in Cuba;Exciting experiences (Floyd Moore);Whistling aboard ship;Ashes on the deck and cleanliness aboard ship;Storms and connected subjects;Ceremonies on crossing the equator, etc.;How to tell a landlubber from a seaman;The true meaning of the word “Scuttlebutt”;Jokes on newcomers (Resemble “left-handed monkey wrench” type);The “Sea Bat”;More Jokes;An Experience of Joseph Fulcher, when sent after a “Monkey Fist”;The three ways to do things;Navy electrical slang vs. that on land;General Navy slang and terminology;Nicknames for Marines;Nicknames for soldiers;Nicknames for Navy Rates;Navy education, particularly pertaining to electronics;Inexperienced ensigns and their mistakes;Sending an ensign to charge a dry cell battery;“All Ahead Bandix”;“The fire party’s on a holiday routine”;The accident in Naples, Italy;The “Rock Ship”;General comments, especially those with regard to senior officers;The ex-enlisted man who rose to a commssion and married a wave officer.

Introduction;Captain Frank Conley and the Sea-Boots;Experiences in Inda;First Mention of Pemmican;White Cat;The Baculuca (Pacific Queen);The Star of India;The Falls of Clyde;The Cadet Ship “Eagle”;Captain Frank Conley expresses the opinion that all Merchant Marine Officers should be required to serve aboard a sailing vessel before receiving their papers;Experiences concering lifeboats and their rations;Conditions on old sailing ships;Standard fare served to sailing ship crews;The coming of unions for Merchant Seamen;Comments on an apporaching schooner;Informant information;How Captain Conley got his ear pierced;More of Captain Conley’s sea experiences and history;More comments on the schooner to the end.

The Archive presents these documents as part of the record of the past and does not endorse the views expressed in these collections.

Title from tape: Spanish Folk Speech * Louise Demont

description_tesim: Side I An old miner named Sam - Delta Material in Dear Old Georgia. Jugo-Slavian Peasant Songs and Dances: Hop Saj Sasa - Croatia; Oj Mladosti Naj Milise Doba - Croatia; Sano Duso (sub-title: Vranjanka) - Serbia (Folkdancer records MH 3020; 78,B); Cacak - Serbia (Folkdancer Records MH 3022; 78,B); Krici Krici Ticek - Croatia; Poskakusa - Croatia; Kolo Kalendara - Croatia; Dance from Pirot - Servia (Sto Mi Omilelo); Makedonka - Serbia (Balkan 547; 78,A); Cujes Mala - Serbia; Kokonjeste - Serbia; Dajchovoto - Serbia; Serbian Medley: Poskok, Ti Momo, Djurdjevka, Igrale Se Delije. Side II Strike Music of American Labor: Hallelujah I'm a Bum (Greenway?); Pie in the Sky; Solidarity Forever; Union Maid - (a dub of the Almanac Singers record); Talking Union - (a dub of the Almanac Singers record); Sister Jenny's Turn to Throw the Ball; Ludlow Massacre - Woody Guthrie; Which Side - Almanacs; Join the NMU; Harry Sims; Fight for Union Recognition (tune: I've been workig on the railroad); The Boatmakers' Union is a No Good Union. Jane Fitzpatrick: An Ould Mon Came A-Courtin'; Oh Dear, What Shall Ee Dae; The Drowned Lover; Unnamed Tune.
Title from tape: Allen * Weide * Fitzpatrick
Performers: Jane Fitzpatrick, Sam (miner, last name unknown).
